framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Mobilizing residents to independently sample water or other environmental exposures when official monitoring is inadequate, then publishing the results with full transparency about methodology.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves the engineer treats public health protection as the actual mission rather than regulatory box-checking, which is exactly what turns a new engineer into the trusted go-to expert during a crisis.

paths.what_it_is: Building and maintaining open-source life-cycle-assessment (LCA) software or modeling frameworks that other practitioners run their environmental impact calculations on.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows technical depth beyond running someone else's spreadsheet model, plus a habit of building durable, reusable infrastructure that is visible and verifiable on GitHub and in citations, not just claimed on a resume.

paths.what_it_is: Designing, field-deploying, and rigorously calibrating low-cost environmental sensor networks (air quality, particulates, gases) against reference-grade instruments, then publishing the calibration methodology openly.
paths.why_it_signals: It bridges hardware, data science, and field engineering to extend monitoring into places expensive regulatory-grade equipment never reaches, a combination that increasingly separates engineers who generate new, usable data from those who only interpret existing data.
